question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env POST endpoint (if exposed) to change hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.frequency to a very large value at runtime, and since the @Scheduled annotation reads frequency only at startup, the change would not take effect but would cause misleading log messages about retry intervals, obscuring the actual polling behavior?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env POST endpoint (if exposed) to set persistBytes=true at runtime, causing the mirror node to suddenly start storing raw record file bytes in the database, potentially exhausting database storage and causing the importer to crash, resulting in missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env POST endpoint (if exposed) to set writeFiles=true at runtime, causing the mirror node to start writing all verified record files to the filesystem, potentially exhausting disk space and causing the importer to crash, resulting in missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordDownloaderProperties.getStreamType() always returns StreamType.RECORD (with path 'recordstreams' and extension 'rcd') to predict the exact cloud storage path structure and enumerate all record files, then use this knowledge to identify gaps in the mirror node's record coverage?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that enabled is a mutable boolean field (not final, not volatile, not AtomicBoolean) in a multi-threaded Spring environment, causing a visibility issue where one thread sets enabled=false but another thread reads the stale cached value of true, leading to inconsistent download behavior and potentially missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that frequency is a mutable Duration field (not final, not volatile) in a multi-threaded environment, causing a thread to read a partially updated Duration object during a runtime configuration refresh, resulting in an invalid polling interval and missed record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between RecordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() and CutoverServiceImpl.isActive() where both blockProperties.isEnabled() and recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() return false simultaneously, causing the cutover service to skip both stream types and produce a window of missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfiguration in CommonDownloaderProperties (accessed via RecordDownloaderProperties.getCommon()) where consensusRatio is set to 1.0, requiring all nodes to agree on a signature, so that if even one node is offline or slow, no record files are ever verified and downloaded, causing all records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonDownloaderProperties.maxSize default of 50 MiB (accessed via RecordDownloaderProperties.getCommon()) to cause record files larger than 50 MiB to be silently skipped, resulting in missing records for high-throughput periods?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileDownloader.shouldDownload() always returns true (hardcoded, unlike AccountBalancesDownloader which checks the repository), meaning the downloader will always attempt to download even when enabled=false is set by the cutover logic, potentially causing conflicting state between the enabled flag and actual download behavior?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the log message in Downloader.downloadAndParseSigFiles() uses downloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis() / 1_000f dynamically, while the actual schedule is fixed at startup, to cause misleading log output that makes operators believe the downloader is polling at a different rate than it actually is, obscuring a misconfiguration that causes missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between RecordDownloaderProperties.isPersistBytes()=false and RecordFileDownloader.getSidecar() where sidecar.setBytes(null) is called when persistBytes is false, causing sidecar file bytes to be discarded after hash verification, making it impossible to re-verify sidecar integrity if the hash verification logic has a bug?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between RecordDownloaderProperties.isWriteFiles()=false and RecordFileDownloader.downloadSidecars() where the sidecar download is skipped entirely when both writeFiles=false and sidecarProperties.isEnabled()=false, causing sidecar records to be permanently missing from the mirror node even if they were produced by the consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordDownloaderProperties.enabled is set to false by CutoverServiceImpl without any persistence to the database, meaning that after a restart the enabled flag reverts to true, causing the record downloader to re-download already-processed record files and potentially create duplicate records in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a cloud storage provider's eventual consistency model combined with the 500ms default frequency to cause the record downloader to list files before a newly uploaded record file is visible, miss it in that poll, and then skip it in the next poll because the lastStreamFile pointer has advanced past it?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to inject a malformed value for hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled via a system property or environment variable, causing a Spring binding exception that prevents the application context from starting and results in no records being downloaded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @NotNull constraint on frequency being the only validation, allowing a Duration of PT0S (zero) to pass validation, causing the @Scheduled task to fire continuously with no delay and overwhelming the thread pool, starving other scheduled tasks and causing record processing to fall behind?
